name: Antigua ciudad de Carmona — circular by Los Alcores
name_original: Antigua ciudad de Carmona — circular por Los Alcores
region_intro: Hiking around Carmona, Spain, offers routes through a landscape characterized by the Los Alcores Ridge and the historic urban center. The terrain includes gentle elevations and paths that often combine natural exploration with cultural sights. This region provides opportunities to explore both the rural surroundings and the ancient architecture of the town. Best hiking trails around Carmona The most popular hiking route is Antigua ciudad de Carmona — circular by Los Alcores, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete, offering views along the ridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Historic Center of Carmona – Carmona Market Hall loop from Carmona, an easy 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This route guides hikers through the town's historic core and past its market hall. Local hikers also love the Los Alcores Ridge loop from Carmona, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through the distinctive Los Alcores Ridge, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Carmona is defined by historic urban paths, ridge walks, and routes that explore the immediate surroundings of the town. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate hikes. The routes in Carmona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Carmona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Carmona?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Carmona, Spain, with options ranging from easy strolls to moderate hikes. You'll find 12 easy routes and 9 moderate ones, ensuring there's something for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Carmona?
A: Yes, Carmona offers 12 easy hiking routes, many of which are suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed walk. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31682759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carmona Market Hall – Historic Center of Carmona loop from Carmona&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 miles (4.6 km) and guides you through the town's historic core.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Carmona?
A: Hiking around Carmona features a mix of historic urban paths, ridge walks along the Los Alcores Ridge, and routes exploring the immediate surroundings of the town. The terrain generally includes gentle elevations, combining natural exploration with cultural sights.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Carmona?
A: Yes, many routes around Carmona are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17265848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Alcores Ridge loop from Carmona&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.0-mile (6.5 km) trail that takes you through the distinctive Los Alcores Ridge and returns to your starting point.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while hiking in Carmona?
A: Many trails in Carmona incorporate the town's rich history. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carmona Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4118677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Gate of Seville&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/385599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jesús Nazareno Bridge / Roman Bridge Alcalá&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Carmona?
A: While specific dog-friendly regulations can vary by trail, generally, many natural paths and rural areas around Carmona are suitable for dogs, especially if kept on a leash. Always check local signage for any restrictions, particularly within urban or protected historical areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Carmona?
A: The hiking routes in Carmona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the combination of natural landscapes with the historical and cultural elements of the town, as well as the varied terrain suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes accessible from Carmona?
A: Yes, Carmona is accessible from the Vía de la Plata, also known as the Silver Route. This impressive long-distance hiking route follows a historic Roman road, offering a significant natural and historical feature for those looking for a more extensive journey.

Q: Are there any routes that combine urban exploration with nature?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Carmona are designed to blend the historic urban center with the surrounding natural landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Carmona – Carmona Market Hall loop from Carmona&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, guiding you through the town's historic core and past its market hall.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Carmona?
A: The best time for hiking in Carmona, Spain, is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, wetter days of winter.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Los Alcores Ridge?
A: Yes, the Los Alcores Ridge is a prominent landscape feature in Carmona. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010639982/antigua-ciudad-de-carmona-circular-by-los-alcores" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antigua ciudad de Carmona — circular by Los Alcores&lt;/a&gt; is a 9.8-mile (15.7 km) trail that offers extensive views along the ridge, providing a great way to experience this distinctive natural area.
tips: The current food market is a work by Ramón del Toro carried out in 1842 in the style of the Castilian main squares, it is a large rectangular patio, measuring 35 by 45 meters, made up of four arcaded galleries and fish markets for the 28 posts, neoclassical style. Only a small section of its corridors correspond to the old conventual cloister.

The Cordoba Gate is one of Carmona's most emblematic monuments and a living testament to its ancient history. It formed part of the walled enclosure and the route of the Via Augusta, connecting Carmona with Cordoba. Its function was more propagandistic than defensive, conceived as a triumphal arch to symbolize the power and prosperity of the Roman Empire.
In the municipality of Carmona is the Cueva de la Batida, a formation with Roman origins that can be perfectly visited from a circular hiking trail that starts from the town itself.
A quiet path that is a greenway that runs below the mountains of Los Alcores. The table that stands on the right side of the path offers views of the area!
Table that is above the rest of the landscape and that can be seen in a southerly direction towards what is called La Campiña. Path that takes you directly to the town of Carmona.
